entry.title: AppRouter path mapping – Custom Domain
entry.content:
Problem

As a follow up on my experience with custom domains, I started applying more custom domains to my applications running on BTP. Feel free to read more about the setup of a custom domain here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/custom-domains-for-btp-cloudfoundry-applications/ba-p/13647389

One of these applications is a CAP application with a UI5 app on top of it and an approuter to access it, the app is published on my GitHub account here: https://github.com/lemaiwo/ReCAPBTPServiceOverview/tree/main

After applying my custom domain to this particular application the approuter added a path behind the url. When I navigated to: https://btp-services.lemaire.tech/ (where “lemaire.tech” is the custom domain with “btp-services” as hostname),

It forwarded me to: https://btp-services.lemaire.tech/recapcapadvancedprogrammingmodelbtpservices/index.html

 

Most of the configuration behind this, was provided by the generators in BAS. In the xs-app.json of the UI5 app the following config was provided:

https://github.com/lemaiwo/ReCAPBTPServiceOverview/blob/main/app/recap.capadvancedprogrammingmodel.btpservices/xs-app.json

In the approuter there were no mappings but it did had a welcomeFile:

https://github.com/lemaiwo/ReCAPBTPServiceOverview/blob/main/approuter/xs-app.json

 Because of this config, the approuter automatically adds the path of the welcomeFile to the url:

This path is based on the name of the application and it is needed for the approuter to forward the user to the right application. In this specific example, I only have one html5 app so that’s why it’s put in the welcomeFile. How this path is generated and accessible by the approuter is very well explaned in this blog post:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-application-router/ba-p/13393550

Especially the part for html5 app integration is important:

Integration with HTML5 App Repo service:


The application router supports seamless integration with the HTML5 Application Repository service. When the application router interacts with HTML5 Application Repository to serve HTML5 Applications, all static content and routes (xs-app.json) are retrieved from HTML5 Application Repository. In case application router needs to route to non HTML5 Applications, it is possible to model that in the xs-app.json of the application router.

To integrate HTML5 Application Repository to an application router based it is required to create an instance of html5-apps-repo service of plan app-runtime and bind it to the application. xs-app.json routes that are used to retrieve static content from HTML5 Application Repository may be modeled in the following format:

 {      "source": "^(/.*)",                                         "target": "$1",                                             "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"                       }


A valid request to application router that uses HTML5 Application Repository must have the following format:

https://<tenantId>.<appRouterHost>.<domain>/<bsPrefix>.<appName-appVersion>/<resourcePath>;

bsPrefix (business service prefix) - Optional

  • It should be used in case the application is provided by a business service bound to this approuter


appName (application name) - Mandatory

  • Used to uniquely identify the application in HTML5 Application Repository persistence
  • Must not contain dots or special characters

appVersion (application version) - Optional

  • Used to uniquely identify a specific application version in HTML5 Application Repository persistence
  • If no version provided, default application version will be used

resourcePath (path to file)

  • The path to the file as it was stored in HTML5 Application Repository persistence

For more details please refer to the following Blog:

Programming applications in Sap Cloud Platform

 More details in this one: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/fiori-application-programming-model-for-cloud-foundry-and-abap-cloud/ba-p/13388142

Solution

As a solution to have a clean url to the application, I applied the following changes to the xs-app.json of the approuter:

  • 1) Changed the welcomeFile to “/index.html”
  • 2) Add a route for the OData service. As I added a route for the app (see next point) which will forward all requests on the root path to the html5 app repo, I also need to add a route before the one of the app specially for the OData. Otherwise all OData requests will be forwarded to the html5 app repository and not the destination.
  • 3) Add a route for the root path directly to the path of the html5 app repository

https://github.com/lemaiwo/ReCAPBTPServiceOverview/blob/main/approuter/xs-app.json

 Make sure the html5 app repository runtime and destination service are bound to the approuter:

In case it is not done yet, add the route in the mta.yaml config:

Result

A clean url with a forward that changes the path: https://btp-services.lemaire.tech/index.html

Full repo: https://github.com/lemaiwo/ReCAPBTPServiceOverview/tree/main

Sidenote: some of the screenshots may still contain a different domain. I recently changed the domain from Wouter.lemaire.tech to Lemaire.tech.

 Kr, Wouter

Hi Lillian,

If your instance is upgraded to the Latest GM by the release, you can revert to Legacy GM in Upgrade Center.

You will find an option showing "Revert to Legacy Goal Management", and you can click "upgrade now" to roll back.
The opt-out is only available for 1H2024 release.
If you upgrade to Latest GM afterwards, you will not see this option in the Upgrade Center any more.

BR,
Yilin

entry.title: Auto Store Integration with SAP EWM
entry.content:
Role of SAP EWM in robotic automation of warehouses:- Before you automate your warehouse, you will need a warehouse management system such as SAP EWM. It has lots of innovative features such as integration with robots or automated storage system. The below picture shows the integration of SAP EWM with an Automated Storage and Retrieval System (ASRS) or Auto Store.

First of all, it is important to understand that the autostore storage system is set up as a black box storage type in SAP EWM. By this, we mean that EWM keeps an overall stock record, but it does not know the exact location of each product within the autostore. The autostore system does keep track of exact contents and location per crate.

During the inbound process, EWM determines which stock must be stored in the autostore system → Upon the receipt in the warehouse, EWM automatically create a warehouse order to the auto store storage type → This warehouse order system will be sent from SAP EWM to the auto store system using IDoc interface → Once, the goods have been physically delivered to the autostore work station, the autostore robot will move the crate with goods to the dedicated place in the grid → Once done, the autostore system will send message to SAP EWM system, which results in the confirmation of the warehouse order → Now SAP EWM knows that the goods are stored in auto store and the autostore system knows exactly in which crate the goods have been stored → When a customer orders a product which is stored in the autostore, a similar process is being triggered → SAP EWM will create a pick warehouse order which is interfaced to autostore → The autostore will determine the location of the product and the robot will pick the crate from the stack and deliver it to the autostore workstation → This will again trigger an IDoc confirmation message to EWM, thus keeping the stock situation between EWM and Autostore aligned.

Inbound Process

Outbound Process

How to setup AutoStore in EWM ?

Connect it as non-SAP sub-system

Set Up IDoc integration for Outbound Mapping (SAP EWM)

  1. In the IMG, choose SCM EWM → Interfaces → Non-SAP system → Connect sub-system.
  2. Set – Link Via ALE Interface against Warehouse number.
  3. Then in the Control IDOC issue node, setup as following –
    Source storage type = from where the stock needs to be moved to Autostore storage type
    Destination storage type = Storage type assigned to Autostore
    WPT = Warehouse process type
    Receiver = EWM Logical System
  4. Then setup the node Function Module IDOC Issue as following –
  • Message type = /SCWM/WMTORD or /SCWM/WMTOCO

OR

search by /SCWM/WM* for the message type

  • Function Module = standard FM like - /SCWM/IDOC_CREATE_WMTOID01

OR

any custom FM but with same interface

  1. Save your entries.

 

Advantages of AutoStore with SAP EWM

  • Fast integration through use of process-safe standard integration.
  • Higher storage capacity, maximum storage density.
  • Minimal space requirements, low traffic area share.
  • Improved space utilization.
  • Fast order processing, fast familiarization for employees. Thanks to familiar, intuitive SAP workstation dialogs.
  • High process transparency.
  • Easily scalable, flexibly expandable.
  • Separately controlled infeed/outfeed prevents waiting times for the storage system or downstream production machines.
  • Low energy consumption.
  • Low operating costs.
  • High availability.

@DataTherapist @PankajLal @APAN1 @Prajith

SAP Inside Track  SAP Extended Warehouse Management  SAP Supply Chain Management EWM - Interfaces SAP Knowledge Warehouse SAP Transportation Management

entry.title: Unpacking the Power of digital transformation using SAP: Key Insights from the Panel Discussion
entry.content:
The panel discussion on digital transformation, SAP tools, and technology's role in modern organizations was an enlightening experience that provided a deep dive into the critical aspects of leveraging technology for business growth. Here's a breakdown of my experience and key takeaways from the discussion:
Technological Advancements and End User Perspectives: The panelists highlighted the importance of understanding technological advancements from an end user perspective, especially in light of the upcoming end of ECC in 2027. The shift towards cloud computing was discussed as a key enabler of digital transformation within SAP environments. The benefits of cloud technology, such as scalability, flexibility, and cost efficiency, were emphasized as driving factors for organizations to adopt cloud-based solutions. The focus on enhancing user experiences through cloud technologies showcased how technology is evolving to meet the changing needs of businesses and users alike, necessitating a future-ready and clean core in SAP.
Adaptation to Digital Transformation: The discussion also touched upon how academic institutions and research bodies are adapting to digital transformation. The investments in digital infrastructure, online platforms, and data analytics tools showcased a strategic approach towards leveraging technology for educational and research purposes. The integration of AI and data analytics in academia highlighted a commitment to staying at the forefront of innovation and providing students and researchers with cutting-edge tools for learning and discovery.
Value Creation through Digital Transformation: Organizations such as Westernacher exemplify the transformative power of digital innovation in supply chain management. Through the strategic implementation of cutting-edge SAP solutions like EWM, and TM, these companies have not only revolutionized customer experiences and harnessed data-driven insights but also orchestrated seamless supply chain operations. This remarkable approach underscores how technology can unlock unparalleled value, elevate competitive advantage, and propel businesses towards innovation and efficiency.
Continuous Improvement and Competitiveness: The panelists emphasized the importance of continuous improvement in digital transformation journeys. Organizations were encouraged to continually refine their digital strategies, operational frameworks, and technological capabilities to remain competitive and meet evolving customer demands. The emphasis on adaptability, innovation, and agility underscored the dynamic nature of digital transformation and the need for organizations to embrace change proactively.

In conclusion, the panel discussion provided valuable insights into how organizations can leverage technology, particularly SAP tools, for business growth and innovation. The emphasis on understanding technological advancements from an end user perspective, adapting to digital transformation in academia, driving value creation through strategic technology adoption, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement highlighted the transformative impact of technology on organizations today.

Panel members: 

  • Manik Paul, Chief Information Officer at Vikram Solar Limited
  • Samarjeet Singh, Chief SAP Innovator at NEC Software Solutions
  • Sabyasachi Mukhopadhyay, Faculty, 2x TEDx Speaker, Teaching Assistant at AI4ICPS, Google Developer Expert in ML, Champion Innovator(Google),
  • Prajith Kizhakkepattu, Warehousing Practice Lead - India & South East Asia (SEA) Region at Westernacher Consulting Pvt. Ltd.
  • Sourav Das, Group Chief of Digital & IT at Rupa & Co.

Panel moderator: 

  • Dipanwita Dutta

 

@DataTherapist 


EWM - Delivery Processing 

SAP Inside Track 

Digital Transformation

entry.title: SIT Kolkata 2024 - An presentation on Cloud Application Programming
entry.content:
Cloud Application Programming

What is Cloud Application Programming?

A framework of libraries, that has a mixture of open-source and SAP technologies. In simple way we can understand CAP as CLOUD APPLICATION PROGRAMMING, a joint combination of all the flavours to bring out robustness, enhanced efficiency, and flexibility.

CAPM offers a more structured and seamless framework for data modelling and enhancing integration with services

KEY - Features:

  • Simplified Development Process for both Frontend and Backend Developers.
  • Due to rich data modelling concept and service implementation, it easily handles large volumes of data.
  • Flexible to allow developers easily adapt to business requirements by extending data models or modification of functionalities or integration with external system.
  • Integration with other SAP technologies like SAP HANA, SAP Cloud Platform and SAPUI5.

How to setup a development environment?

  • We can install the Business Application Studio to do a setup. We then require a Cloud Platform Account to access the same.
  • Install CAP tools extension for the Visual Basic Studio Code.
  • Install SAP Fiori Tools Extension.
  • Install HANA database (optional).
  • Configure GIT Repository.

First we need to open our Trial Account at BTP cockpit. Then we need to add services from the marketplace , assign roles to the respective user, then goto Business Application Studio and click Go To Application as shown in the image

Setting up the Environment

 

Once The BAS opens we find a screen like this , where we can select the CAP project and everything gets setup from the backend services.

 

Click the selected options

Select the options

The project gets generated 

Please install required environment from the Terminal which does all the job and sets up the environment

Execute the program using Terminal. 

 

 

 

Like this we can develop our applications without switching to multiple editors.

 

@DataTherapist

entry.title: Re: journal entry FC exchange rate
entry.content:
Hi,

We cannot see what JE entry has been added and where the Exchange Rate is not defined with a screengrab.

FC entry will be recorded to either a All currencies G/L Account/BP or a FC Only G/L Account/BP code. In case of FC Only G/L Account/BP, the value in the FC Debit and FC Credit is calculated automatically based on the Exchange Rate.

For All Currencies G/L Account/BP Code, it is possible to have a blank FC values. And if the entry is posted, then the same cannot be amended as JE is a legally binding transaction and changes to it are for limited fields.

If you want, you can cancel and create a new one with correct values in the FC fields as required.

Also, you can check the KBA 3169760 which explains how a SC/FC Only can be added for the required balances.

Kr,

Jitin
